diagrams.description: Sociology is the scientific study of society, social behavior, and social institutions. This flowchart shows how the discipline aims to understand and explain social patterns, structures, and change by integrating core elements: major theoretical perspectives (Functionalism, Conflict Theory, Symbolic Interactionism, Feminist Theory), research methods (surveys, ethnography, experiments, comparative-historical analysis), and foundational concepts (social structure, culture, socialization, stratification, power). These components guide analysis at both micro and macro levels, ultimately producing outcomes that inform public policy, promote social justice, and deepen our comprehension of human behavior.
